overrides.override_summary: Post-termination covenant not to compete may be unenforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may not be bound by the 1-year/5-mile noncompete after leaving the system

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration in MN and litigation venue in MN may be unenforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate/arbitrate in California rather than traveling to Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: General release may not waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law or Franchise Relations Act. Includes Civil Code 1542 waiver language in release form.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees retain statutory protections even after signing a general release

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of punitive damages may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to seek punitive damages despite contractual waiver

overrides.override_summary: Highest interest rate allowed by law in California is 10% annually (vs 14% in agreement)
overrides.why_it_matters: Late charges capped at 10% instead of 14% for CA salons

overrides.override_summary: Application of MN laws and forum may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchise law may govern the relationship instead of Minnesota law

overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment shall waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects CA franchisees from being forced to waive fraud-in-the-inducement claims

overrides.override_summary: Upon termination or non-renewal, franchisee must be compensated for fair market value of certain assets per Haw. Rev. Stat. 482E-6(3)
overrides.why_it_matters: HI franchisees receive compensation upon exit, unlike in most other states

overrides.override_summary: All initial franchise fees and pre-opening payments deferred until franchisor meets material pre-opening obligations and salon opens
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects HI franchisees from paying fees before franchisor delivers on pre-opening commitments

overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment shall waive claims under state franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard anti-waiver protection for Hawaii franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the franchise relationship for IL franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act provides additional protections beyond federal FTC Rule

overrides.override_summary: Litigation/arbitration in Illinois or as mutually agreed
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees do not need to travel to Minnesota for dispute resolution

overrides.override_summary: General release does not waive rights under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain statutory franchise law protections even after signing releases

overrides.override_summary: Fees deferred until pre-opening obligations met and salon opens
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects MD franchisees from paying fees before franchisor delivers

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause. 30-day written notice required with opportunity to cure.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be terminated without good cause — stronger protection than base agreement

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must fairly compensate franchisee for FMV of inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures upon non-renewal (if term < 5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing similar business or no 6-month advance notice)
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees may receive compensation if franchise is not renewed

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is prohibited
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees can resolve disputes in Michigan rather than Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Transfer cannot be refused without good cause (defined to include failure to meet qualifications, competitor status, refusal to comply, unpaid debts)
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees have stronger transfer rights

overrides.override_summary: Minnesota franchise law protections apply
overrides.why_it_matters: Home state protections for the franchisor's state; Minnesota Franchise Act provides specific franchisee protections

overrides.override_summary: Extensive additional disclosures required. Franchisor cannot use negotiation to impose less favorable terms than in FDD.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees get stronger disclosure protections and cannot be negotiated down from FDD terms

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota franchise law protections apply
overrides.why_it_matters: ND has strong franchisee protection statutes

overrides.override_summary: Rhode Island franchise law protections apply
overrides.why_it_matters: RI Franchise Investment Act provides additional protections

overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment shall waive claims under applicable state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: VA franchisees retain statutory protections against fraud and misrepresentation

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law prohibits termination, cancellation, non-renewal, or substantial change without good cause. 90-day prior written notice required. 60-day cure period.
overrides.why_it_matters: WI franchisees have among the strongest termination protections in the US — 90-day notice with 60-day cure, and good cause required
